title: Israel x Hezbollah ceasefire by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if there is an official ceasefire agreement, defined as a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in direct military engagement, between Israel and Hezbollah by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET.

If the agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to “Yes,” regardless of whether the ceasefire officially takes effect after the resolution date.

Any form of informal understanding, backchannel communication, de-escalation without an announced agreement, or unilateral pause in hostilities will not be considered an official ceasefire. Humanitarian pauses, limited operational pauses, or temporary tactical stand-downs will not count toward the resolution of this market.

A broader peace deal, normalization agreement, or political framework will qualify only if it includes a publicly announced and mutually agreed halt in military engagement between the Israel and Hezbollah, effective on a specified date, or otherwise confirmed by an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting. Agreements that outline future negotiations or de-escalation measures without an explicit, dated commitment to stop fighting will not qualify.

This market’s resolution will be based on official statements from the Israeli Government and Hezbollah. However, a wide consensus of credible media reporting confirming that an official ceasefire agreement has been reached will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: A US-brokered 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon took effect on April 16, 2026, halting hostilities with Hezbollah militants in southern Lebanon amid the ongoing 2026 Lebanon war, following rare direct diplomatic talks in Washington hosted by Secretary of State Marco Rubio. This temporary de-escalation, announced by President Trump, aims to facilitate negotiations toward a permanent peace agreement, with a potential White House summit between Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and the Lebanese president on the horizon. Hezbollah, not formally party to the deal, insists on restrictions against Israeli freedom of movement, while protests in northern Israel highlight domestic opposition; minor violations and sticking points like disarmament and border withdrawals underscore the truce's fragility ahead of its April 26 expiration.

title: Magic vs. Pistons
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 19 at 6:30PM ET:
If the Magic win, the market will resolve to "Magic".
If the Pistons win, the market will resolve to "Pistons".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

markets.question: Magic vs. Pistons: O/U 222.5
markets.description:
In the NBA game, scheduled for April 19 at 6:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Magic and Pistons combine to score 223 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 223, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series, the eighth-seeded Orlando Magic stunned the top-seeded Detroit Pistons 112-101 on the road at Little Caesars Arena, with Paolo Banchero posting 23 points, nine rebounds and four assists amid Orlando's stifling defense that extended Detroit's home postseason losing streak. The Magic, who advanced via a dominant 121-90 play-in tournament rout of the Hornets on April 18, also beat the Pistons 123-107 in their April 6 regular-season finale, signaling strong momentum and matchup edge despite Detroit's league-best 60-22 record. Pre-game, Jonathan Isaac was questionable for Orlando with a left knee sprain, but the Pistons entered healthy including Cade Cunningham post-injury recovery; Game 2 returns to Detroit, where home-court advantage and bounce-back urgency could shift series dynamics for the 1-0 underdogs.

title: 2026 FIFA World Cup Winner
description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain and France share 16.1% implied probabilities as co-favorites in the trader consensus for the 2026 FIFA World Cup winner, reflecting their dominant UEFA qualifying campaigns—Spain topped their group unbeaten while France secured direct qualification with ease—and favorable group draws announced early April: Spain in H alongside Uruguay, Cape Verde, and Saudi Arabia; France in I with Senegal and Norway. Recent March friendlies underscored parity, with Spain's 3-0 rout of Serbia, England's 1-1 draw versus Uruguay, and Germany's 4-3 thriller over Switzerland, highlighting no runaway leader amid the expanded 48-team format's top-two-plus-best-third advancement structure. Tight odds persist due to uncertainties in player fitness, pre-tournament form, and host advantages for USA, Canada, and Mexico ahead of the June kickoff.

title: Trail Blazers vs. Spurs
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for April 19 at 9:00PM ET:
If the Trail Blazers win, the market will resolve to "Trail Blazers".
If the Spurs win, the market will resolve to "Spurs".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: In Game 1 of the Western Conference first-round playoffs at Frost Bank Center, the No. 2 seed San Antonio Spurs hold home-court advantage over the No. 7 Portland Trail Blazers, who clinched via a 114-110 play-in win over Phoenix Tuesday. Blazers star Damian Lillard remains out with a season-ending Achilles tear, but their injury report is otherwise immaculate—the healthiest all season per official updates. Spurs rule reserve Jordan McLaughlin out (ankle), preserving a deep rotation around Victor Wembanyama. San Antonio won two of three regular-season matchups, including 112-101 on April 8, underscoring superior form, rest edge, and matchup history shaping trader consensus.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Charlotte Hornets win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Oklahoma City Thunder lead trader consensus for the 2026 NBA Champion at 48.5% implied probability after clinching the West's top seed with a league-best 64-18 regular-season record and routing the Suns 119-84 in Game 1 of their first-round playoff series, showcasing Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber play and Chet Holmgren's defensive growth. San Antonio Spurs sit second at 15.8% following their franchise-record 62-20 mark as the No. 2 West seed, bolstered by Victor Wembanyama's dominance amid an 11-game win streak earlier in April. Boston Celtics hold third at 13.2% with a 56-26 record and a 123-91 Game 1 rout of the 76ers, leveraging Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown's scoring despite Detroit Pistons' surprising No. 1 East seed but lower market odds amid tougher projected matchups. Denver Nuggets' 8.5% reflects their solid 54-28 finish and 12-game win streak late in the season. No major injuries have shifted rotations in the last 48 hours.

title: Counter-Strike: Spirit vs Vitality (BO5) - IEM Rio Playoffs
description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Spirit and Vitality in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 19 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit win the match against Vitality.

This market will resolve to "Vitality" if Vitality win the match against Spirit.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Map Handicap: TS (-2.5) vs Vitality (+2.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between Spirit and Vitality in the IEM Rio Playoffs, initially scheduled for April 19 at 1:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Spirit" if Spirit wins 3 or more maps than Vitality in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Vitality". 

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vitality entered the IEM Rio 2026 grand final BO5 as the consensus favorite against Spirit, driven by their flawless playoff run with dominant wins over Natus Vincere and FURIA, plus a season of three prior S-tier titles positioning them for a historic second ESL Grand Slam. Spirit advanced via upsets against MOUZ and Falcons from a tougher bracket side, fueled by donk's explosive tournament form (1.35+ rating, high ADR on aggressive entries) and a new team psychologist for mental resilience. The map veto—featuring Spirit picks Mirage and Dust2 alongside Vitality's Nuke and Overpass—highlighted stylistic clashes, with Vitality's superior CT execution and H2H edge (10-4 recent) anchoring trader sentiment, though Spirit's upset potential loomed in the high-pressure LAN decider.

title: Fed decision in April?
description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Federal Reserve's April 2026 meeting.

If the target federal funds rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meeting scheduled for April 28-29, 2026 according to the official calendar: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm.

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve at https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the FOMC’s statement for their April meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders overwhelmingly price a 99.4% implied probability of no change in the federal funds rate at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting, reflecting consensus on the Federal Reserve's cautious stance amid sticky inflation and resilient labor conditions. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year—up from 2.4% in February—with energy prices surging 12.5%, while nonfarm payrolls added 178,000 jobs, exceeding soft landing expectations and tempering rate-cut bets. The March FOMC held the 3½–3¾% target range, with minutes emphasizing data dependence amid geopolitical uncertainties. This positioning could be challenged by sharper-than-expected April labor softening or a surprise inflation cooldown ahead of the decision.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom leads trader consensus at 27.5% implied probability for the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination on Polymarket, buoyed by his executive experience as California governor, national fundraising surge since late 2025, and high-profile clashes with the Trump administration that elevated his profile ahead of his term ending in 2027. Recent polls like McLaughlin & Associates (April 8-15) show Kamala Harris surging to 29% after her April 10 signal of considering another run post-2024 loss, yet markets diverge, pricing her at 6.7% amid lingering nominee baggage. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez holds 8.3% on progressive appeal and youth, while Jon Ossoff (6.5%) gains from swing-state Georgia incumbency. Consolidation could hinge on 2026 midterm outcomes for governors like Josh Shapiro and Gretchen Whitmer, early primary state polling in Iowa and New Hampshire, and factional endorsements from Biden-era moderates or the progressive wing.

title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Iran and the United states agree to a permanent peace deal by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”. 

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

A permanent peace deal refers to any agreement which explicitly indicates that military hostilities between the United States and Iran have ended or will permanently cease, or uses equivalent language clearly signaling a lasting end to military hostilities between the United States and Iran. Agreements that are explicitly temporary or which do not include a definitive agreement to end military hostilities between the US and Iran on a lasting basis (e.g. a temporary extension of the two-week ceasefire agreement announced on April 7, 2026), will not qualify.

A qualifying agreement will be considered to have been established if either of the following conditions are met:

- The United States and Iran each sign or formally adopt a written agreement (e.g. a treaty or multi-point agreement) which meets the above criteria.

- Both the governments of the United States and Iran provide clear public confirmation that a qualifying agreement has been definitively established. Negotiations, statements of progress, or other statements which do not constitute a definitive announcement that a qualifying agreement has been reached will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US-Iran negotiations for a permanent peace deal remain stalled after the April 8 ceasefire in the 2026 Iran war, mediated by Pakistan, with indirect talks in Islamabad concluding last weekend without agreement on core issues like nuclear suspension, sanctions relief, and Strait of Hormuz access. President Trump expressed optimism yesterday for resumed discussions in Pakistan as early as Monday, citing Iran's 10-point proposal as a basis, though Iran's deputy foreign minister stated Saturday no date is set. Recent US seizure of an Iranian-flagged vessel enforcing the naval blockade underscores ongoing tensions. Traders watch for diplomatic breakthroughs amid escalation risks, with historical precedents like the JCPOA showing such deals often take months amid mutual distrust.

title: F1 Drivers' Champion
description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' W17 car has dominated the opening three Grands Prix of the 2026 season under new regulations, propelling teammates George Russell and rookie Kimi Antonelli to the top of the drivers' standings and driving trader consensus toward them as title favorites. Antonelli leads with 72 points after back-to-back wins in China and Japan, edging Russell (63 points), who opened with victory in Australia; their 1-2 finishes have built a commanding Mercedes lead in the constructors' too. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (49 points, third) trails with consistent podiums but lacks pace, while Red Bull's Max Verstappen languishes ninth on 12 points amid reliability woes. Recent Japan GP momentum favors Antonelli's charge, though Russell's experience keeps him narrowly ahead in implied probabilities, reflecting the intense intra-team battle ahead of Miami.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vice President JD Vance holds a slim lead in trader consensus at 19% implied probability for the 2028 presidential election winner, buoyed by his position as heir apparent in the Trump administration and a dominant 53% in the March 2026 CPAC straw poll, despite his approval rating hitting historic lows for a VP amid policy headwinds. California Governor Gavin Newsom trails closely at 17%, reflecting Democratic frontrunner status in fragmented primary fields post-2024, with a late-March UMass Lowell head-to-head poll showing Vance edging him 33%-30%. Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 11% gains from foreign policy visibility, including recent Iran negotiations highlighting GOP divides. The tight race underscores uncertainty ahead of 2026 midterms, which could reshape incumbency advantages, fundraising, and battleground turnout, with primaries starting in 2027 as key separators.

title: Iran x Israel/US conflict ends by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a continuous 14-day period without any qualifying military action between Iran, and Israel and the United States that begins at any time between market creation and the specified end date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No". 

The 14-day period may begin at any time between the creation of this market and the specified end date (ET), and must continue uninterrupted through 12:00 PM ET on the 14th calendar day after the strike is confirmed. 

A "military action" is defined as any use of force by Iran, or Israel and the United States against the other’s soil, or official embassies or consulates, that is either officially acknowledged by the acting government or confirmed through a clear consensus of credible reporting. 

This includes, but is not limited to, airstrikes, naval attacks, and ground incursions. 

Cyberattacks, sanctions, and diplomatic actions do not count. 

Only actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory will qualify as Iranian military actions. Attacks on Israel or the US by proxy forces (i.e. Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: A fragile US-brokered ceasefire between Iran, Israel, and the US, effective since mid-April, nears expiration on April 22 amid mutual accusations of violations, driving trader focus on potential escalation or diplomatic breakthrough. In the past 48 hours, US forces seized an Iranian-flagged tanker attempting to skirt the naval blockade of Iranian ports, prompting Tehran's vow of swift retaliation, while President Trump announced second-round talks in Islamabad today over sticking points like the Strait of Hormuz—conditionally reopened but now re-blockaded by Iran—and nuclear safeguards. A separate Lebanon ceasefire holds, but no comprehensive end to hostilities is confirmed, with Iran's Supreme Leader warning of further defeats for US and Israeli forces; upcoming negotiations and Hormuz developments will be pivotal.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Republican Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican Party sources.

Any replacement of the Republican nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket slightly favors Robert F. Kennedy Jr. at 49% implied probability for the 2028 Republican presidential nomination, edging Vice President J.D. Vance at 39.1%, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 21.1% amid a fragmented field testing post-Trump GOP succession dynamics. RFK Jr.'s lead stems from his high-profile HHS role advancing Make America Healthy Again initiatives, bolstered by a recent April 4 midterm travel push as a Trump surrogate and family speculation of his 2028 ambitions, surging his odds to 49% in early March. Vance holds strong via CPAC straw poll victories and heir-apparent status, while Rubio gains from donor preferences over Vance in Trump polling. The tight race reflects MAGA base loyalty splits and awaits 2026 midterm results, explicit Trump endorsements, or primary polling to create separation.

title: Eurovision Winner 2026
description:
This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

This market will resolve to the country whose candidate for Eurovision 2026 wins.

If at any point it is impossible for the listed candidate to win Eurovision 2026 based on the rules of the competition (i.e. they are eliminated), this market may immediately resolve to "No".

If no winner is announced by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve "Other". All ties will be broken according to EBU's official Eurovision rules.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Eurovision (https://eurovision.tv/), including live footage of Eurovision 2026, however a consensus of credible reporting will suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen top trader consensus at 37% implied probability to win Eurovision 2026 in Vienna, propelled by their explosive UMK national final victory with "Liekinheitin"—a high-energy duet blending violin flair and pop hooks that's hailed as a staging powerhouse, dominating bookmakers since early April. France's teenage soprano Monroe follows at 11.3% with the elegant ballad "Regarde!", buoyed by critical praise for her vocal poise, while Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund holds 10.7% on the momentum of his February DMGP triumph with catchy electronic pop "Før Vi Går Hjem". Recent national final wins, like Greece's Akylas ("Ferto") on April 7, have tightened the field, but Finland leads amid OGAE fan polls and betting surges. With more selections pending and semis looming May 12-14, jury-televote splits remain a wildcard in this unpredictable contest.

title: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?
description: What price will Bitcoin hit in April?

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $60,000 in April?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final Low price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT Low prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: NVIDIA commands a 99.2% implied probability of remaining the world's largest company by market capitalization at the end of April, reflecting its dominant $4.97 trillion valuation as of mid-April—far ahead of Alphabet at around $4 trillion and Apple near $3.8 trillion. This trader consensus stems from NVIDIA's unchallenged leadership in AI accelerators, fueled by explosive demand for its H100 and Blackwell GPUs in data centers amid the ongoing artificial intelligence boom, with recent quarterly results showing sustained revenue growth exceeding 60%. Saudi Aramco lags at roughly $1.7 trillion due to softer oil dynamics. Challenges could arise from a broader tech sell-off disproportionately hitting NVIDIA's premium valuation, surprise regulatory scrutiny on AI chip exports, or blockbuster product announcements from rivals like Alphabet's next Gemini model iteration propelling a rapid catch-up before April 30 close.

title: Colombia Chamber of Representatives Election: 3rd Place
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the third-greatest number of seats in the Colombian Chamber of Representatives as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The named parties or coalitions will be primarily ranked by the number of seats won in the specified election. If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

If two or more parties are tied on seats, ties will be broken by the total number of valid votes received, with higher vote totals ranking higher. If parties remain tied, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the listed party abbreviations. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the third-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party or coalition in the Chamber of Representatives.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Partido Liberal Colombiano (PLC) for third-most seats in Colombia's Chamber of Representatives following the March 8, 2026, parliamentary elections, reflecting official tallies showing Historic Pact for Colombia (PH) with 42 seats, Centro Democrático (CD) with 30, and PLC with 26—ahead of Partido Conservador Colombiano's 19. Final counts from the Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil, completed in recent weeks without major disputes, have locked in this ordering despite special circumscription allocations for indigenous, Afro-Colombian, and international voters. PH's slim odds stem from its commanding lead, while CD trails PLC narrowly but securely in second; low turnout (around 50%) and fragmented vote amplified traditional parties' proportional gains under departmental list rules. Presidential primaries on the same ballot previewed May 31 dynamics but did not alter legislative math.

title: LoL: Bilibili Gaming vs Anyone's Legend (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for April 19 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against Anyone's Legend.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Quadra Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Quadra Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Quadra Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Any Player Penta Kill?
markets.description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Bilibili Gaming and Anyone's Legend. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

markets.question: First Blood in Game 1?

eventMetadata.context_description: Bilibili Gaming enters as Group Ascend frontrunners at 5-1 series record in LPL Split 2's double round-robin BO3 format, bolstered by elite top laner Bin, mid laner Knight, and newcomers Xun and Viper delivering consistent macro dominance despite a recent 0-2 loss to leader Top Esports four days ago. Anyone's Legend languishes at 2-3, buoyed by a clean 2-0 over Team WE last week but undermined by shaky early laning and jungler Tarzan's suboptimal CS efficiency amid analyst critiques of their crisis-level form. With top-four direct playoff qualification on the line, BLG's superior head-to-head history and depth shape trader consensus toward their edge in this pivotal group stage clash, though AL's desperation could spark map-specific upsets in the current patch meta.

title: Dota 2: Team Spirit vs Team Falcons (BO3) - PGL Wallachia Group Stage
description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Round 2 match between Team Spirit and Team Falcons in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit win the match against Team Falcons.

This market will resolve to "Team Falcons" if Team Falcons win the match against Team Spirit.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.description:
In the upcoming Round 2 match between Team Spirit and Team Falcons in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 8:20AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game Handicap: TS (-1.5) vs Team Falcons (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 Round 2 match between Team Spirit and Team Falcons in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 8:20AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit wins 2 or more games than Team Falcons in this match.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Team Falcons". 

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the handicap, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

markets.question: Game 1: Ends in Daytime?
markets.description:
This market is about whether Game 1 ends during the daytime phase of the Dota 2 day/night cycle. In Dota 2, the day/night cycle alternates every 5 minutes (daytime runs from 0:00–5:00, 10:00–15:00, 20:00–25:00, and so on, while nighttime runs from 5:00–10:00, 15:00–20:00, 25:00–30:00, and so on).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the game-ending action (Ancient destruction or midgame forfeit) occurs during a daytime phase of the base in-game clock. This market will resolve to "No" if the game ends during a nighttime phase. Temporary nighttime effects caused by hero abilities (e.g., Night Stalker's Dark Ascension) do not affect resolution, only the base day/night cycle timer is considered.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

markets.question: Game 1: Both Teams Beat Roshan?
markets.description:
This market is about whether both teams kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. Roshan is a powerful neutral boss in Dota 2 that spawns at the start of the game and respawns 8–11 minutes after each death, dropping the Aegis of the Immortal and other valuable items.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Spirit and Team Falcons each kill Roshan at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not kill Roshan during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

In the upcoming Round 2 match between Team Spirit and Team Falcons in the PGL Wallachia Group Stage, initially scheduled for April 19 at 8:20AM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 1.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 1 is 45 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

eventMetadata.context_description: Team Falcons hold a slight edge in trader consensus for their BO3 group stage clash against Team Spirit at PGL Wallachia Season 8 Swiss stage, reflecting Falcons' momentum from a Round 1 victory over Team Yandex and strong adaptation to the current Dota 2 patch favoring their versatile hero pool around skiter and Malr1ne. Spirit, entering 1-0 after an early win, boasts TI pedigree and Yatoro's carry prowess but has flashed inconsistencies in recent drafts amid mixed Season 7 results. Head-to-head splits evenly from March encounters, with execution, mid-lane supremacy, and macro play pivotal amid Swiss format pressures—losses heighten elimination risks in the $1M Tier-1 event's Bucharest LAN.

title: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a 98.8% implied probability against the Iranian regime falling by April 30, reflecting its institutional resilience following the US-Iran war that began in late February, Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death on March 1, and the smooth transition to Mojtaba Khamenei as successor by early March. Security forces suppressed earlier 2025-2026 protests amid economic woes, while a fragile April 7 ceasefire—despite recent US port blockades, Strait of Hormuz closures, and vessel seizures—has held without sparking mass uprisings or IRGC defections, as confirmed by US intelligence assessments. With just 10 days remaining, structural entrenchment outweighs pressures from sanctions and diplomacy. Realistic shifts could stem from sudden nationwide protests, leadership splintering, or war escalation overwhelming controls.

title: Elon Musk # tweets April 14 - April 21, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from April 14 12:00 PM ET to April 21, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Elon Musk posting 220-239 tweets from April 14-21 (39.5% implied probability), with 240-259 close behind at 25%, reflecting his steady cadence of 25-35 daily posts amid viral cultural debates. This positioning stems from sustained high activity over the past week, including April 14-16 barrages criticizing South Africa's race-based laws as "Apartheid 2.0," Grok AI's top benchmark wins, Tesla Semi and Cybercab promotions, and political commentary on U.S. justice reforms. Recent bursts, like early April 20 engagement on anti-White rhetoric and family birthday shoutouts, reinforce the pace, though no major catalysts have spiked beyond norms. With April 21 remaining, traders eye potential late surges from breaking news or X Spaces for final tallies.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of April?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls (“Arrivals of Ships”) for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and April 30, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls equal to or above the specified level, or once data has been published for the final date in the specified period and no such value has been published. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. However, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from qualifying. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for April 30, 2026, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 75.5% implied probability against Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic normalizing by April 30, reflecting persistent geopolitical tensions from U.S. naval blockades of Iranian ports and Tehran's selective transit controls amid the ongoing war. Ship-tracking data from Kpler and MarineTraffic reveals daily vessel transits languishing below 10% of pre-crisis norms—around 140 ships versus single digits or brief spikes like 20-35 on April 18—following rapid reversals after ceasefires, including recent U.S. seizures of Iranian vessels and retaliatory drone strikes. With just 10 days remaining, profound uncertainty in restoring oil and LNG flows through this critical energy chokepoint sustains elevated war-risk insurance premiums and route diversions, pending diplomatic breakthroughs or escalations.

title: NFL Champion 2027
description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2027 NFL league championship. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2027 NFL league championship per the rules of the NFL (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2027 NFL league championship game is cancelled, postponed after March 31, 2027 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from NFL (https://www.nfl.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending Super Bowl LX champion Seattle Seahawks lead trader consensus at 11.5% implied probability for Super Bowl LXI victory, driven by roster continuity, a record-setting early extension for a key contributor, and top-ranked defensive DVOA from their championship run, positioning them for a repeat amid NFC West parity. Los Angeles Rams follow closely at 9.5% after recent odds tightening to +750 amid aggressive free agency splashes and Matthew Stafford's health outlook, bolstering their NFC contender status. Buffalo Bills sit third at 7.5%, leveraging Josh Allen's elite quarterback play, a favorable schedule projection, and consistent AFC playoff pedigree, while the wide-open field under 12% tops reflects draft uncertainty, lingering injury recoveries, and offseason realignments like quarterback additions across contenders.

title: US x Iran diplomatic meeting by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile US-Iran ceasefire nearing expiration, the first direct high-level talks since 1979 concluded inconclusively in Islamabad on April 12 after marathon sessions led by US Vice President JD Vance and Iranian officials, stalling over Iran's refusal to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz amid US port blockades. President Trump announced Saturday that US negotiators will arrive in Pakistan on April 21 for a second round, with Pakistani mediators shuttling between Tehran and Washington to avert escalation. Defense officials signal military readiness if no deal emerges soon, while sticking points include nuclear enrichment limits, sanctions relief, and unrestricted Hormuz shipping—key factors shaping trader focus on imminent diplomacy versus renewed conflict.

title: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?
description: What will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit in April 2026?

markets.question: Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $160 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for the Active Month of WTI Crude Oil futures has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For WTI futures, the active month refers to the nearest listed contract month. The active month changes at 6:00:00 PM ET at the start of the trading session two business days prior to that contract's last trading day, at which point the next listed contract becomes the active month.

For WTI Crude Oil (CL) futures, the last trading day is defined as three business days prior to the 25th calendar day of the month preceding the contract's delivery month (or four business days prior if the 25th calendar day is not a business day), consistent with CME contract specifications.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session for the underlying market will be considered. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours as listed on Pyth.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If the Active Month contract does not trade at all during the listed time frame, this market will resolve to "No".

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the underlying market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Active Month WTI Crude Oil futures "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore?search=WTI, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil futures have surged past $100 per barrel mid-April 2026 amid escalating Middle East tensions, including U.S.-Israel actions against Iran and Strait of Hormuz risks, before pulling back to the mid-$90s on de-escalation signals and looming supply agreements. This volatility reflects trader pricing in a geopolitical risk premium offset by OPEC+ gradual output hikes of 206,000 barrels per day starting April, alongside mixed U.S. EIA data showing a 0.9 million barrel inventory draw for the week ending April 10 after prior builds. Global refining margins remain elevated per the latest IEA report, supporting prices despite softening demand outlooks. Traders eye this week's EIA report for the week ending April 17 and any OPEC+ policy tweaks as pivotal before month-end resolution.

title: Canadiens vs. Lightning
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 19 at 5:45PM ET:
If the Canadiens win, the market will resolve to "Canadiens".
If the Lightning win, the market will resolve to "Lightning".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Montreal Canadiens hold a 1-0 series lead over the Tampa Bay Lightning after Juraj Slafkovsky's hat trick, including the overtime power-play winner, fueled a stunning 4-3 Game 1 road victory at Amalie Arena on April 19. The resilient Canadiens, who went 2-1-1 against Tampa Bay in the regular season despite finishing third in the Atlantic Division with 106 points, overcame an early deficit behind Samuel Montembeault's goaltending. Lightning, the No. 2 seed with matching points and home-ice advantage, scratched Victor Hedman (personal) and missed Pontus Holmberg (upper-body), while Montreal lacks Noah Dobson (upper-body) for the round. Game 2 looms Tuesday in Tampa, where Andrei Vasilevskiy and the Bolts seek a split before the series shifts north, testing both teams' depth amid playoff intensity.

title: Atlanta Braves vs. Philadelphia Phillies
description:
In the upcoming MLB game between the Atlanta Braves and Philadelphia Phillies, scheduled for April 19 at 7:20PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Atlanta Braves" if the Atlanta Braves win the game.

This market will resolve to "Philadelphia Phillies" if the Philadelphia Phillies win the game.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, or ends in a tie, this market will resolve 50-50.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Philadelphia Phillies". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Philadelphia Phillies".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

To know when a postponed game will be played, please check the home team's schedule on MLB.com for the listed team and look for the game described as a makeup game.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official final statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 24 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Atlanta Braves hold a commanding NL East lead at 14-7 after sweeping recent games over the Phillies, including a 9-0 rout powered by Austin Riley's two homers and a 3-1 win yesterday, fueling trader optimism amid their booming bats. However, the Braves' rotation remains severely depleted by injuries—Spencer Strider sidelined with an oblique strain, Spencer Schwellenbach and others post-elbow surgery—leaving Grant Holmes (3.32 ERA) vulnerable in matchups like Sunday's against Andrew Painter. Phillies, hampered by Zack Wheeler's blood clot absence and bullpen strains (Pop, Bowlan, Duran), lean on Harper, Turner, Schwarber, and Bohm at home in Citizens Bank Park, where early-season struggles and home-field edge create upset potential in this heated rivalry.

title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by...?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Kharg Island is no longer under Iranian control by March 31, 2026 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

“No longer under the control of Iran” means that Iran no longer exercises primary governmental or military control over Kharg Island, and another state, occupying force, or internationally backed authority has established control over the island.

Temporary raids, isolated landings, special operations, bombardment, sabotage, naval presence offshore, or temporary disruption of Iranian activity will not qualify on their own.

An announcement, threat, or claim that Iran has lost control will not qualify without actual control being established.

If control changes pursuant to a negotiated settlement, ceasefire term, surrender, or transfer agreement, this will qualify only once actual control has been established on the island.

If control over Kharg Island is contested, unclear, disputed, or not sufficiently established by the resolution date, this will not qualify, and the market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official statements from the relevant governments and militaries, along with a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kharg Island, Iran's critical Persian Gulf oil export terminal handling nearly 90% of its crude shipments, remains under full Iranian control following US airstrikes on military targets there on April 7, 2026, which avoided oil infrastructure. Tehran reported no damage and has reinforced defenses with additional air systems, mines, and troops since late March amid US threats of a potential ground seizure to compel reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. Satellite imagery as of April 16 shows tankers still loading despite the ongoing US naval blockade. Escalation risks, including amphibious operations or stalled diplomatic talks, represent key uncertainties ahead of market resolution dates.

title: Who will be confirmed as Fed Chair?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next individual formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve.

Formal confirmation as Chair of the Federal Reserve requires the Senate to confirm a nominee as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Recess appointments without Senate confirmation will not count. Senate confirmation of a listed individual as a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors will not alone qualify.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

If no Senate confirmation for the position of Chair of the Federal Reserve has occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's formal nomination of former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh as the next Fed Chair in early March, transmitted to the Senate on March 4, has solidified trader consensus at 94.4% implied probability for his confirmation, reflecting Republican control of the Senate Banking Committee and momentum toward a full Senate vote ahead of Jerome Powell's May 15 term expiration. Recent developments, including the scheduling of Warsh's confirmation hearing for April 21 despite Democratic efforts to delay over financial disclosures exceeding $100 million and a probe into Powell, underscore advancing procedural hurdles. While commanding, this lead could be challenged by unexpected bipartisan opposition, a key Republican hold like Sen. Thom Tillis's prior block, scandal revelations, or Trump withdrawing the nomination for an alternative.
